title: President Truman's Promise to Taiwan
story: Tensions were high in 1950 as conflict brewed in Asia. On July 27, US President Harry Truman made a bold move. He promised to send help to Taiwan, an island nation off China's coast. This pledge of support showed America's commitment to protecting its allies in the region.
activity_question: What did President Truman promise to Taiwan?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important for the US to support Taiwan?

title: Austria's Freedom Day
story: Cheers erupted across Austria as the country regained its full independence in 1955. For a decade after World War II, four powerful nations had controlled different parts of Austria. But on July 27, the last foreign troops left, and Austrians celebrated their newfound freedom. It was a peaceful end to a long occupation, marking a fresh start for the nation.
activity_question: What happened in Austria on July 27, 1955?
follow_up_question: How do you think Austrians celebrated their newfound freedom?

title: Guiding Planes Through the Skies
story: Pilots soared into a new era of flight in 1920. On July 27, the radio compass was used for the first time to guide aircraft. This clever device helped pilots navigate by picking up radio signals from the ground. It made flying safer and more precise, allowing planes to find their way even when they couldn't see landmarks below.
activity_question: What device was used for the first time to guide aircraft?
follow_up_question: How do you think the radio compass made flying safer?

title: The Mysterious Vanishing Ship
story: A ghostly fog swallowed the SS Waratah as it sailed from Durban to Cape Town in 1909. On July 27, the British ship vanished without a trace, taking all aboard with it. Despite extensive searches, no wreckage or survivors were ever found. The disappearance of the Waratah remains one of the sea's greatest mysteries.
activity_question: What was the name of the ship that vanished?
follow_up_question: Why do you think ships sometimes disappear without a trace?

title: A Brilliant Mind Bubbles Up
story: Loránd Eötvös, a Hungarian scientist with big ideas, entered the world in 1848. Born in Buda, he grew up to become a famous physicist. Eötvös made waves by introducing the concept of molecular surface tension. His work helped us understand how liquids behave, from tiny droplets to vast oceans. Today, we celebrate the birth of this curious mind on July 27.
activity_question: What scientific concept did Loránd Eötvös introduce?
follow_up_question: How might understanding surface tension help us in everyday life?

title: Sparking Discoveries: John Hopkinson's Birth
story: Zap! A future electrical genius entered the world. John Hopkinson, born in Manchester in 1849, would grow up to become a brilliant physicist and engineer. His work with electricity led to Hopkinson's Law, an important principle in the field. Though his life was cut short, Hopkinson's spark of innovation continues to light up the world of science on July 27.
activity_question: What important principle did John Hopkinson develop?
follow_up_question: Can you think of any electrical devices you use that might rely on Hopkinson's work?

title: Atomic Scientist's Birthday Surprise
story: Boom! A future atomic scientist entered the world. Kenneth Bainbridge was born in Cooperstown, New York on July 27, 1904. He grew up to become a brilliant physicist, making important discoveries about nuclear isotopes. Bainbridge's work helped unlock secrets of the atom, paving the way for advances in science and technology.
activity_question: What field of science did Kenneth Bainbridge work in?
bonus_fact: Cooperstown is famous for the National Baseball Hall of Fame, founded in 1939.

title: The Scientist Who Sniffed Out Radon
story: A curious baby named Friedrich Ernst Dorn took his first breath in 1848. Born in a place now called Warmia, Poland, he grew up to become a brilliant physicist. Dorn made a groundbreaking discovery that changed how we understand radioactivity. He found that radium gives off an invisible gas called radon. This discovery on July 27 helped scientists learn more about the mysteries of atoms.
activity_question: What did Friedrich Ernst Dorn discover?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to study invisible gases like radon?

title: Queen Mother Moore: A Civil Rights Champion
story: Born in Louisiana on July 27, 1898, Queen Mother Moore became a powerful voice for African-American rights. She dedicated her life to fighting for equality and helping others. Moore's passion for justice made her a respected leader in the civil rights movement, inspiring many to stand up for what's right.
activity_question: What cause did Queen Mother Moore dedicate her life to?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to stand up for equality?
